source_pr.title: planner, statistics: allow (auto) analyze single partition in dynamic-only mode
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

sub-PR for the [PR#22472](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/22472)

Problem Summary:

1. analyze single partition is not allowed in dynamic-only mode.
2. auto analyze is global level (collect data from all partitions and update global stats) in dynamic-only mode, which is hard to trigger.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
1. allow analyze single partition in dynamic-only mode.
2. set auto analyze to partition level in dynamic-only mode.

This sub-PR depends on other sub-PRs of the [PR#22472](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/22472).

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- allow (auto) analyze single partition in dynamic-only mode<!-- Please write a release note here to describe the change you made when it is released to the users of TiDB. If your PR doesn't involve any change to TiDB(like test enhancements, RFC proposals...), you can write `No release note`. -->

The patch does not provide a clear flaky failure signature or a narrowly scoped stabilization change that can be confidently mapped to a root-cause category, so we mark it as needing more evidence.
